perturbation.original_text: 3.2 QUARTERLY FEE PAYMENTS. Sponsor will pay Snap the quarterly fees as set forth below on Exhibit A hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 QUARTERLY FEE PAYMENTS. Sponsor will pay Snap the quarterly fees as set forth below on Exhibit A hereto. All quarterly fees must be paid within 15 days of the invoice date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by adding a specific payment deadline (15 days) to the quarterly fee payments, while Exhibit A only lists the payment amounts and dates without any mention of this 15-day deadline. This contradicts the original text and creates uncertainty regarding the payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
EXHIBIT A
PAYMENTS AND EXCLUSIVITY
1. The Exclusive Category is "PROVIDERS OF TRAVEL SERVICE".
2. The up-front payment called for by Section 3.1 will be [*].
3. Sponsor shall pay the following non-accrued obligation quarterly fees on or prior to the following dates:
June 30, 1999 [*]
September 30, 1999 [*]
December 31, 1999 [*]
March 31, 2000 [*]
June 30, 2000 [*]
September 30, 2000 [*]
December 31, 2000 [*]
perturbation.justification: The original text (3.2) only refers to Exhibit A for quarterly fee amounts and dates. The changed text adds a 15-day payment deadline from the invoice date, which is not mentioned in Exhibit A. Exhibit A specifies payment dates (e.g., June 30, 1999). The introduction of the 15-day deadline directly contradicts the specified dates in Exhibit A because if an invoice is issued on June 20, the payment would be due July 5, conflicting with the June 30 date. This creates an inconsistency as it's unclear whether the payment is due on the date specified in Exhibit A or within 15 days of the invoice date, leading to a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 TERM. The Term shall commence on the date of this Agreement and, unless earlier terminated or extended as provided below, shall end as of December 31, 2000.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 TERM. The Term shall commence on the date of this Agreement and, unless earlier terminated or extended as provided below, shall end as of December 31, 2000, unless Snap provides written notice to extend the term for an additional year, in which case the term will automatically extend to December 31, 2001.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction by stating the term automatically extends if Snap provides written notice. Section 4.2 states that either party can terminate the term with 60 days prior written notice, thus causing a contradiction on weather the contract will automatically be extended or terminated if the notice given.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 TERMINATION. Either party may terminate the Term upon not less than sixty (60) days prior written notice to the other party of any breach hereof by such other party. In addition, either party may terminate the Term immediately upon written notice in the event that the other party shall fail to do business in the normal course or become subject to any bankruptcy, assignment for creditors, corporate dissolution or similar proceeding.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4.1 introduces a contradiction with Section 4.2. Section 4.1 now states that the term automatically extends if Snap provides written notice, whereas Section 4.2 allows either party to terminate the term with 60 days prior written notice. This creates a direct conflict as to whether the contract will automatically extend or be terminated if written notice is given, fulfilling the definition of inconsistencies leading to in-text contradiction. The obligation in 4.1 regarding automatic extension directly interferes with the right to terminate in 4.2, resulting in a strong contradiction that impacts enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 8.7 NOTICES. Any notice or other communication required or permitted to be given hereunder shall be given in writing and delivered by first class U.S. mail, in person, or mailed via confirmed facsimile or e-mail, or delivered by courier service, properly addressed and stamped with the required postage, to the person signing this agreement on behalf of the applicable party at its address specified above and shall be deemed effective upon receipt.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.7 NOTICES. Any notice or other communication required or permitted to be given hereunder shall be given in writing and delivered by first class U.S. mail, in person, or mailed via confirmed facsimile or e-mail, or delivered by courier service, properly addressed and stamped with the required postage, to the person signing this agreement on behalf of the applicable party at its address specified above. However, all notices related to termination of this agreement must be delivered exclusively via courier service to be considered valid.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a specific requirement for termination notices (courier service only), while the first sentence allows for multiple methods of delivery for all notices, including termination notices. This makes it unclear which method is valid for termination and can be disputed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.7 NOTICES. Any notice or other communication required or permitted to be given hereunder shall be given in writing and delivered by first class U.S. mail, in person, or mailed via confirmed facsimile or e-mail, or delivered by courier service, properly addressed and stamped with the required postage, to the person signing this agreement on behalf of the applicable party at its address specified above. However, all notices related to termination of this agreement must be delivered exclusively via courier service to be considered valid.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text (and contradicted text) states that any notice can be delivered by various methods, including courier. The changed text then specifies that termination notices must *exclusively* be delivered via courier service. This directly contradicts the initial allowance of multiple delivery methods for all notices, including termination notices, rendering the agreement unclear on the valid methods for termination notice, adhering to the definition of inconsistencies.
